crypto.c(3) Library Functions Manual crypto.c(3)

NAME

crypto.c - 🔐 Client cryptography: handshake integration, X25519 key exchange, and per-session encryption

SYNOPSIS

Functions


void client_crypto_set_mode (uint8_t mode)
Set crypto mode for handshake (encryption + authentication). int client_crypto_init (void)
Initialize client crypto handshake. int client_crypto_handshake (acip_transport_t *transport)
Perform crypto handshake with server. bool crypto_client_is_ready (void)
Check if crypto handshake is ready. const crypto_context_t * crypto_client_get_context (void)
Get crypto context for encryption/decryption. int crypto_client_encrypt_packet (const uint8_t *plaintext, size_t plaintext_len, uint8_t *ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_size, size_t *ciphertext_len)
Encrypt a packet for transmission. int crypto_client_decrypt_packet (const uint8_t *ciphertext, size_t ciphertext_len, uint8_t *plaintext, size_t plaintext_size, size_t *plaintext_len)
Decrypt a received packet. void crypto_client_cleanup (void)
Cleanup crypto client resources. bool crypto_client_should_rekey (void)
Check if session rekeying should be triggered. int crypto_client_initiate_rekey (void)
Initiate session rekeying (client-initiated). int crypto_client_process_rekey_request (const uint8_t *packet, size_t packet_len)
Process received REKEY_REQUEST packet from server. int crypto_client_send_rekey_response (void)
Send REKEY_RESPONSE packet to server. int crypto_client_process_rekey_response (const uint8_t *packet, size_t packet_len)
Process received REKEY_RESPONSE packet from server. int crypto_client_send_rekey_complete (void)
Send REKEY_COMPLETE packet to server and commit to new key.

Detailed Description

🔐 Client cryptography: handshake integration, X25519 key exchange, and per-session encryption

CORE RESPONSIBILITIES:

1.
Initialize client crypto context with authentication credentials
2.
Perform cryptographic handshake with server during connection
3.
Manage global crypto context for client connection
4.
Provide encryption/decryption functions for secure packet transmission
5.
Support multiple authentication modes (password, SSH key, passwordless)
6.
Handle session rekeying for long-lived connections

CRYPTOGRAPHIC HANDSHAKE ARCHITECTURE:

The handshake follows a multi-phase protocol:

PHASE 0: PROTOCOL NEGOTIATION:

  • Step 0a: Send client protocol version
  • Step 0b: Receive server protocol version
  • Step 0c: Send client crypto capabilities
  • Step 0d: Receive server crypto parameters

PHASE 1: KEY EXCHANGE:

  • Step 1: Receive server's ephemeral public key and send our public key
  • Client generates ephemeral key pair for this session
  • Both sides derive shared secret using X25519 key exchange

PHASE 2: AUTHENTICATION:

  • Step 2: Receive auth challenge and send response
  • Client signs challenge with identity key (if client has identity key)
  • Server verifies client identity (if whitelist enabled)
  • Step 3: Receive handshake complete message

CRYPTO INITIALIZATION:

The client supports three initialization modes:

1.
SSH KEY MODE (--key specified):
•
Parses Ed25519 private key from file or gpg:keyid format
•
Extracts public key for authentication
•
Supports password-protected keys (SSH agent or prompt)
•
Optional password for dual authentication (key + password)
2.
PASSWORD MODE (--password specified):
•
Uses Argon2id key derivation from shared password
•
Both client and server derive same key from password
•
No identity keys required
3.
PASSWORDLESS MODE (no credentials):
•
Generates random ephemeral keys
•
No long-term identity (no authentication)
•
Suitable for trusted networks or testing

GLOBAL CRYPTO CONTEXT:

The client uses a single global crypto context (g_crypto_ctx):

  • Shared across all connection attempts (reused on reconnection)
  • Initialized once per program execution
  • Cleaned up on program shutdown
  • Stores server connection info for known_hosts verification

SERVER IDENTITY VERIFICATION:

Client verifies server identity using known_hosts:

  • Checks server's identity key against ~/.ascii-chat/known_hosts
  • First connection: Prompts user to accept server key
  • Subsequent connections: Verifies key matches stored value
  • Key mismatch: Warns user about potential MITM attack
  • Optional --server-key for explicit server key verification

CLIENT AUTHENTICATION REQUIREMENTS:

When server requires client authentication (whitelist enabled):

  • Client must provide identity key with --key option
  • Client's public key must be in server's --client-keys list
  • Authentication failure results in connection rejection
  • Interactive prompt warns user if no identity key provided

SESSION REKEYING:

Long-lived connections support periodic rekeying:

  • crypto_client_should_rekey(): Checks if rekey is needed
  • crypto_client_initiate_rekey(): Client-initiated rekeying
  • crypto_client_process_rekey_request(): Handles server-initiated rekey
  • Rekeying refreshes encryption keys without reconnecting

ENCRYPTION/DECRYPTION OPERATIONS:

After handshake completion:

  • crypto_client_encrypt_packet(): Encrypts packets before transmission
  • crypto_client_decrypt_packet(): Decrypts received packets
  • Both functions use global crypto context
  • Automatic passthrough when encryption disabled (--no-encrypt)

ALGORITHM SUPPORT:

The client currently supports:

  • Key Exchange: X25519 (Elliptic Curve Diffie-Hellman)
  • Cipher: XSalsa20-Poly1305 (Authenticated Encryption)
  • Authentication: Ed25519 (when client has identity key)
  • Key Derivation: Argon2id (for password-based authentication)
  • HMAC: HMAC-SHA256 (for additional integrity protection)

ERROR HANDLING:

Handshake errors are handled appropriately:

  • Server disconnection during handshake: Log and return error
  • Protocol mismatch: Log detailed error and abort connection
  • Authentication failure: Log and return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED
  • Network errors: Detect and handle gracefully (reconnection)
  • Invalid packets: Validate size and format before processing

THREAD SAFETY:

Crypto operations are thread-safe:

  • Global crypto context protected by initialization flag
  • Single crypto context per client process (no concurrent handshakes)
  • Encryption/decryption operations are safe for concurrent use
  • Rekeying operations coordinate with connection thread

INTEGRATION WITH OTHER MODULES:

  • main.c: Calls client_crypto_init() during client startup
  • server.c: Calls client_crypto_handshake() during connection
  • protocol.c: Uses encryption functions for secure packet transmission
  • crypto/handshake.h: Core handshake protocol implementation
  • crypto/keys/keys.h: Key parsing and management functions
  • crypto/known_hosts.h: Server identity verification

WHY THIS MODULAR DESIGN:

Separating cryptographic operations from connection management provides:

  • Clear cryptographic interface for application code
  • Easier handshake protocol evolution
  • Better error isolation and debugging
  • Improved security auditing capabilities
  • Independent testing of crypto functionality

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

October 2025

Version

2.0

See also

server.c For connection establishment and Crypto Module handshake timing

crypto/handshake.h For handshake protocol implementation

crypto/keys/keys.h For key parsing and management

crypto/known_hosts.h For server identity verification

Definition in file src/client/crypto.c.

Function Documentation

int client_crypto_handshake (acip_transport_t * transport)

Perform crypto handshake with server. Perform crypto handshake with server

Parameters

transport Client's persistent transport (wraps the connected socket)

Returns

0 on success, -1 on failure

Definition at line 434 of file src/client/crypto.c.

434                                                          {
435   socket_t socket = acip_transport_get_socket(transport);
436   // If client has --no-encrypt, skip handshake entirely
437   if (GET_OPTION(no_encrypt)) {
438     log_debug("Client has --no-encrypt, skipping crypto handshake");
439     return 0;
440   }
441 
442   // If we reach here, crypto must be initialized for encryption
443   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_crypto_lc)) {
444     log_error("Crypto not initialized but server requires encryption");
445     log_error("Server requires encrypted connection but client has no encryption configured");
446     log_error("Use --key to specify a client key or --password for password authentication");
447     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED; // No retry - configuration error
448   }
449 
450   log_debug("Starting crypto handshake with server...");
451 
452   START_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
453 
454   // Step 0a: Send protocol version to server
455   protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
456   client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
457   client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
458   client_version.supports_encryption = g_crypto_mode;    // Send crypto mode bitmask
459   client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;             // No compression for now
460   client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
461   client_version.feature_flags = 0;
462 
463   int result = send_protocol_version_packet(socket, &client_version);
464   if (result != 0) {
465     log_error("Failed to send protocol version to server");
466     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
467     return -1;
468   }
469   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Protocol version sent successfully");
470 
471   // Step 0b: Receive server's protocol version
472   packet_type_t packet_type;
473   void *payload = NULL;
474   size_t payload_len = 0;
475 
476   result = receive_packet(socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
477   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
478     log_error("Failed to receive server protocol version (got type %u)", packet_type);
479     log_error("Packet type 0x%x (decimal %u) - Expected 0x%x (decimal %d)", packet_type, packet_type,
480               PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION, PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION);
481     log_error("This suggests a protocol mismatch or packet corruption");
482     log_error("Raw packet type bytes: %02x %02x %02x %02x", (packet_type >> 0) & 0xFF, (packet_type >> 8) & 0xFF,
483               (packet_type >> 16) & 0xFF, (packet_type >> 24) & 0xFF);
484     if (payload) {
485       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
486     }
487     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
488     return -1;
489   }
490 
491   if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
492     log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu, expected %zu", payload_len,
493               sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
494     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
495     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
496     return -1;
497   }
498 
499   protocol_version_packet_t server_version;
500   memcpy(&server_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
501   buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
502 
503   // Convert from network byte order
504   uint16_t server_proto_version = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_version);
505   uint16_t server_proto_revision = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_revision);
506   uint8_t server_mode = server_version.supports_encryption;
507 
508   log_info("Server protocol version: %u.%u (crypto mode: 0x%02x)", server_proto_version, server_proto_revision,
509            server_mode);
510 
511   // Verify server echoed our mode
512   if (server_mode != g_crypto_mode) {
513     log_error("Server mode mismatch: got 0x%02x, expected 0x%02x", server_mode, g_crypto_mode);
514     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
515     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
516   }
517 
518   // Step 0c: Send crypto capabilities to server
519   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Sending crypto capabilities");
520   crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps = {0};
521   client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(KEX_ALGO_X25519);
522   client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 | AUTH_ALGO_NONE);
523   client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305 | CIPHER_ALGO_NONE);
524   client_caps.requires_verification = 0; // Client doesn't require server verification (uses known_hosts)
525   client_caps.preferred_kex = KEX_ALGO_X25519;
526   client_caps.preferred_auth = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_AUTH(g_crypto_mode) ? AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 : AUTH_ALGO_NONE;
527   client_caps.preferred_cipher =
528       ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(g_crypto_mode) ? CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305 : CIPHER_ALGO_NONE;
529 
530   result = send_crypto_capabilities_packet(socket, &client_caps);
531   if (result != 0) {
532     log_error("Failed to send crypto capabilities to server");
533     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
534     return -1;
535   }
536   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Crypto capabilities sent successfully");
537 
538   // Step 0d: Receive server's crypto parameters
539   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Receiving server crypto parameters");
540   payload = NULL;
541   payload_len = 0;
542 
543   result = receive_packet(socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
544   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS) {
545     log_error("Failed to receive server crypto parameters (got type %u)", packet_type);
546     if (payload) {
547       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
548     }
549     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
550     return -1;
551   }
552 
553   if (payload_len != sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t)) {
554     log_error("Invalid crypto parameters packet size: %zu, expected %zu", payload_len,
555               sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
556     buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
557     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
558     return -1;
559   }
560 
561   crypto_parameters_packet_t server_params;
562   memcpy(&server_params, payload, sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
563   buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
564 
565   // Convert from network byte order
566   uint16_t kex_pubkey_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_params.kex_public_key_size);
567   uint16_t auth_pubkey_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_params.auth_public_key_size);
568   uint16_t signature_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_params.signature_size);
569   uint16_t shared_secret_size = NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_params.shared_secret_size);
570 
571   log_debug("Server crypto parameters: KEX=%u, Auth=%u, Cipher=%u (key_size=%u, auth_size=%u, sig_size=%u, "
572             "secret_size=%u, verification=%u)",
573             server_params.selected_kex, server_params.selected_auth, server_params.selected_cipher, kex_pubkey_size,
574             auth_pubkey_size, signature_size, shared_secret_size, server_params.verification_enabled);
575   log_debug("Raw server_params.kex_public_key_size = %u (network byte order)", server_params.kex_public_key_size);
576 
577   // Set the crypto parameters in the handshake context
578   result = crypto_handshake_set_parameters(&g_crypto_ctx, &server_params);
579   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
580     FATAL(result, "Failed to set crypto parameters");
581   }
582 
583   // Store verification flag - server will verify client identity (whitelist check)
584   // This is independent of whether server provides its own identity
585   if (server_params.verification_enabled) {
586     g_crypto_ctx.server_uses_client_auth = true;
587     g_crypto_ctx.require_client_auth = true;
588     log_info("Server will verify client identity (whitelist enabled)");
589   }
590 
591   // Validate that server chose algorithms we support
592   if (server_params.selected_kex != KEX_ALGO_X25519) {
593     log_error("Server selected unsupported KEX algorithm: %u", server_params.selected_kex);
594     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
595     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
596   }
597 
598   // Validate cipher selection based on negotiated mode
599   bool expect_cipher = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(g_crypto_mode);
600   if (expect_cipher && server_params.selected_cipher != CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305) {
601     log_error("Server selected unsupported cipher algorithm: %u", server_params.selected_cipher);
602     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
603     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
604   }
605   if (!expect_cipher && server_params.selected_cipher != CIPHER_ALGO_NONE) {
606     log_error("Server chose cipher %u but client requested no encryption", server_params.selected_cipher);
607     STOP_TIMER("client_crypto_handshake");
608     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
609   }
610 
611   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Protocol negotiation completed successfully");
612 
613   // Step 0.5: Send CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO with expected server key (multi-key support)
614   // This allows the server to select the correct identity key from g_server_identity_keys[]
615   if (g_crypto_ctx.verify_server_key && strlen(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key) > 0) {
616     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Sending expected server key to support multi-key selection");
617 
618     // Parse expected server key to get the Ed25519 public key
619     public_key_t expected_keys[MAX_CLIENTS];
620     size_t num_expected_keys = 0;
621 
622     if (parse_public_keys(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key, expected_keys, &num_expected_keys, MAX_CLIENTS) == 0 &&
623         num_expected_keys > 0) {
624       // Send the first expected key (client should only have one expected server key)
625       // Format: 32-byte Ed25519 public key
626       log_debug("Sending CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO with expected server key for multi-key selection");
627       result = send_packet(socket, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO, expected_keys[0].key, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
628 
629       if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
630         FATAL(result, "Failed to send CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO packet");
631       }
632 
633       // Log the key fingerprint for debugging
634       char hex_key[65];
635       pubkey_to_hex(expected_keys[0].key, hex_key);
636       log_debug("Sent expected server key: %s", hex_key);
637     } else {
638       log_warn("Failed to parse expected server key '%s' - server will use default key",
639                g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key);
640     }
641   }
642 
643   // Step 1: Receive server's public key and send our public key
644   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Starting key exchange");
645   {
646     packet_type_t packet_type;
647     uint8_t *payload = NULL;
648     size_t payload_len = 0;
649     int recv_result = receive_packet(socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
650     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
651       FATAL(recv_result, "Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT from server");
652     }
653     // Client handshake functions take ownership of payload
654     result = crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(&g_crypto_ctx, transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
655   }
656   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
657 #ifdef _WIN32
658     // On Windows: Cleanup capture resources before exiting to prevent Media Foundation threads from hanging exit()
659     // Media Foundation creates background COM threads that can block exit() if not properly shut down
660     capture_cleanup();
661 #endif
662     FATAL(result, "Crypto key exchange failed");
663   }
664   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Key exchange completed successfully");
665 
666   // SECURITY: Warn when server requires client verification but client has no identity key
667   bool client_has_identity = (g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519);
668 
669   if (g_crypto_ctx.require_client_auth && !client_has_identity) {
670     // Server requires client verification but client has no identity key
671     log_warn("Server requires client verification but client has no identity key");
672 
673     // Check if we're running interactively (stdin is a terminal)
674     // In debug builds with CLAUDECODE, skip interactive prompts (LLM can't type)
675 #ifndef NDEBUG
676     bool skip_interactive = platform_getenv("CLAUDECODE") != NULL;
677 #else
678     bool skip_interactive = false;
679 #endif
680     if (!skip_interactive && platform_is_interactive()) {
681       // Interactive mode - prompt user for confirmation
682       // Lock terminal for the warning message
683       bool previous_terminal_state = log_lock_terminal();
684 
685       log_plain("\n"
686                 "@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@\n"
687                 "@  WARNING: CLIENT AUTHENTICATION REQUIRED                                    @\n"
688                 "@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@@\n"
689                 "\n"
690                 "The server requires client authentication (--client-keys enabled),\n"
691                 "but you have not provided a client identity key with --key.\n"
692                 "\n"
693                 "To connect to this server, you need to:\n"
694                 "  1. Generate an Ed25519 key: ssh-keygen -t ed25519\n"
695                 "  2. Add the public key to the server's --client-keys list\n"
696                 "  3. Connect with: ascii-chat client --key /path/to/private/key\n");
697 
698       // Unlock before prompt (prompt_yes_no handles its own terminal locking)
699       log_unlock_terminal(previous_terminal_state);
700 
701       // Prompt user - default is No since this will likely fail
702       if (!platform_prompt_yes_no("Do you want to continue anyway (this will likely fail)", false)) {
703         log_plain("Connection aborted by user.");
704         exit(0); // User declined - exit cleanly
705       }
706 
707       log_plain("Warning: Continuing without client identity key (connection may fail).\n");
708     } else {
709       // Non-interactive mode (background/script) - just log warning and continue
710       log_warn("Non-interactive mode: Continuing without client identity key (connection may fail)");
711     }
712   }
713 
714   // Step 2: Receive auth challenge and send response
715   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO: Sending auth response to server...");
716   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Starting auth response");
717   {
718     packet_type_t packet_type;
719     uint8_t *payload = NULL;
720     size_t payload_len = 0;
721     int recv_result = receive_packet(socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
722     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
723       FATAL(recv_result, "Failed to receive auth challenge from server");
724     }
725     // Client handshake functions take ownership of payload
726     result = crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(&g_crypto_ctx, transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
727   }
728   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
729     FATAL(result, "Crypto authentication failed");
730   }
731   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO: Auth response sent successfully");
732   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Auth response completed successfully");
733 
734   // Check if handshake completed during auth response (no authentication needed)
735   if (g_crypto_ctx.state == CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
736     // Propagate encryption flag to crypto context
737     g_crypto_ctx.crypto_ctx.encrypt_data = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(g_crypto_mode);
738     // Set crypto context on the persistent transport now that handshake is complete
739     transport->crypto_ctx = (crypto_context_t *)crypto_client_get_context();
740     STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(debug, 100 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "client_crypto_handshake",
741                        "Crypto handshake completed successfully (no authentication)");
742     return 0;
743   }
744 
745   // Step 3: Receive handshake complete message
746   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Waiting for handshake complete message");
747   {
748     packet_type_t packet_type;
749     uint8_t *payload = NULL;
750     size_t payload_len = 0;
751     int recv_result = receive_packet(socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
752     if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
753       FATAL(recv_result, "Failed to receive handshake completion from server");
754     }
755     // Client handshake functions take ownership of payload
756     result = crypto_handshake_client_complete(&g_crypto_ctx, transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
757   }
758   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
759     FATAL(result, "Crypto handshake completion failed");
760   }
761 
762   // Propagate encryption flag to crypto context
763   g_crypto_ctx.crypto_ctx.encrypt_data = ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT(g_crypto_mode);
764   // Set crypto context on the persistent transport now that handshake is complete
765   transport->crypto_ctx = (crypto_context_t *)crypto_client_get_context();
766 
767   STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG(debug, 100 * NS_PER_MS_INT, "client_crypto_handshake", "Crypto handshake completed successfully");
768   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE: Handshake completed successfully, state=%d", g_crypto_ctx.state);
769   return 0;
770 }




References ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_AUTH,
    ACIP_CRYPTO_HAS_ENCRYPT, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    AUTH_ALGO_ED25519, AUTH_ALGO_NONE,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::auth_public_key_size,
    buffer_pool_free(), capture_cleanup(),
    CIPHER_ALGO_NONE, CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305,
    protocol_version_packet_t::compression_algorithms,
    protocol_version_packet_t::compression_threshold,
    CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED, crypto_client_get_context(),
    acip_transport::crypto_ctx,
    crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(),
    crypto_handshake_client_complete(),
    crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(),
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY, crypto_handshake_set_parameters(),
    ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, FATAL,
    protocol_version_packet_t::feature_flags, g_crypto_ctx,
    GET_OPTION, HOST_TO_NET_U16, KEX_ALGO_X25519,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::kex_public_key_size,
    KEY_TYPE_ED25519, lifecycle_is_initialized(),
    log_debug, log_error, log_info,
    log_lock_terminal(), log_plain, log_unlock_terminal(),
    log_warn, MAX_CLIENTS, NET_TO_HOST_U16,
    NS_PER_MS_INT, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_CLIENT_HELLO,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS, PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION,
    parse_public_keys(), platform_getenv(),
    platform_is_interactive(), platform_prompt_yes_no(),
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_auth,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_cipher,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::preferred_kex,
    protocol_version_packet_t::protocol_revision,
    protocol_version_packet_t::protocol_version, pubkey_to_hex(),
    receive_packet(),
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::requires_verification,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_auth,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_cipher,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::selected_kex,
    send_crypto_capabilities_packet(), send_packet(),
    send_protocol_version_packet(),
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::shared_secret_size,
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::signature_size, START_TIMER,
    STOP_TIMER, STOP_TIMER_AND_LOG,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_auth_algorithms,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_cipher_algorithms,
    crypto_capabilities_packet_t::supported_kex_algorithms,
    protocol_version_packet_t::supports_encryption, and
    crypto_parameters_packet_t::verification_enabled.


Referenced by client_main(),
    connection_attempt_tcp(), and
  server_connection_establish().






int
  client_crypto_init (void )


Initialize client crypto handshake. Initialize client crypto
    handshake




Returns


0 on success, -1 on failure


Definition at line 209 of file
  src/client/crypto.c.


209                              {
210   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Starting crypto initialization");
211 
212   // Reset if already initialized (allows reinit on reconnection)
213   if (lifecycle_reset(&g_crypto_lc)) {
214     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Already initialized, resetting for reinit");
215     crypto_handshake_destroy(&g_crypto_ctx);
216   }
217 
218   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Initializing crypto handshake context");
219   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: crypto_mode=%d (NONE=0, ENCRYPT=1, AUTH=2, FULL=3)", g_crypto_mode);
220 
221   // Check if we have an SSH key, password, or neither
222   int result;
223   bool is_ssh_key = false;
224   private_key_t private_key;
225 
226   // Get string options safely
227   const options_t *opts = options_get();
228   const char *encrypt_key = opts && opts->encrypt_key[0] != '\0' ? opts->encrypt_key : "";
229   const char *password = opts && opts->password[0] != '\0' ? opts->password : "";
230   const char *address = opts && opts->address[0] != '\0' ? opts->address : "localhost";
231   int port = opts ? opts->port : OPT_PORT_INT_DEFAULT;
232   const char *server_key = opts && opts->server_key[0] != '\0' ? opts->server_key : "";
233 
234   // Load client private key if provided via --key
235   if (strlen(encrypt_key) > 0) {
236     // --key supports file-based authentication (SSH keys, GPG keys via gpg:keyid)
237 
238     // For SSH key files (not gpg:keyid format), validate the file exists
239     if (strncmp(encrypt_key, "gpg:", 4) != 0) {
240       if (validate_ssh_key_file(encrypt_key) != 0) {
241         return -1;
242       }
243     }
244 
245     // Parse key (handles SSH files and gpg:keyid format)
246     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Loading private key for authentication: %s", encrypt_key);
247     if (parse_private_key(encrypt_key, &private_key) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
248       log_debug("Successfully parsed SSH private key");
249       log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Parsed key type=%d, KEY_TYPE_ED25519=%d", private_key.type, KEY_TYPE_ED25519);
250       is_ssh_key = true;
251     } else {
252       log_error("Failed to parse SSH key file: %s", encrypt_key);
253       log_error("This may be due to:");
254       log_error("  - Wrong password for encrypted key");
255       log_error("  - Unsupported key type (only Ed25519 is currently supported)");
256       log_error("  - Corrupted key file");
257       log_error("");
258       log_error("Note: RSA and ECDSA keys are not yet supported");
259       log_error("To generate an Ed25519 key: ssh-keygen -t ed25519");
260       return -1;
261     }
262   }
263 
264   if (is_ssh_key) {
265     // Use SSH private key for authentication
266     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Using SSH key for authentication");
267 
268     // Initialize crypto context (generates ephemeral X25519 keys)
269     result = crypto_handshake_init("crypto_client_local", &g_crypto_ctx, false); // false = client
270     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
271       FATAL(result, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake");
272     }
273 
274     // Store the Ed25519 keys for authentication
275     memcpy(&g_crypto_ctx.client_private_key, &private_key, sizeof(private_key_t));
276 
277     // Extract Ed25519 public key from private key
278     g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
279     memcpy(g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.key, private_key.public_key, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE);
280     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.comment, private_key.key_comment,
281                  sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.client_public_key.comment) - 1);
282 
283     // Extract GPG key ID if this is a GPG key (format: "gpg:KEYID")
284     if (strncmp(encrypt_key, "gpg:", 4) == 0) {
285       const char *key_id = encrypt_key + 4;
286       size_t key_id_len = strlen(key_id);
287       // Accept 8, 16, or 40 character GPG key IDs (short/long/full fingerprint)
288       if (key_id_len == 8 || key_id_len == 16 || key_id_len == 40) {
289         SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id, key_id, sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id));
290         log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Extracted client GPG key ID (%zu chars): %s", key_id_len,
291                   g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id);
292       } else {
293         log_warn("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Invalid GPG key ID length: %zu (expected 8, 16, or 40)", key_id_len);
294         g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id[0] = '\0';
295       }
296     } else {
297       // Not a GPG key, clear the field
298       g_crypto_ctx.client_gpg_key_id[0] = '\0';
299     }
300 
301     // SSH key is already configured in the handshake context above
302     // No additional setup needed - SSH keys are used only for authentication
303 
304     // Clear the temporary private_key variable (we've already copied it to g_crypto_ctx)
305     sodium_memzero(&private_key, sizeof(private_key));
306 
307     // If password is also provided, derive password key for dual authentication
308     if (strlen(password) > 0) {
309       log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Password also provided, deriving password key");
310       crypto_result_t crypto_result = crypto_derive_password_key(&g_crypto_ctx.crypto_ctx, password);
311       if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
312         log_error("Failed to derive password key: %s", crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
313         return -1;
314       }
315       g_crypto_ctx.crypto_ctx.has_password = true;
316       log_debug("Password authentication enabled alongside SSH key");
317     }
318 
319   } else if (strlen(GET_OPTION(password)) > 0) {
320     // Password provided - use password-based initialization
321     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Using password authentication");
322     result = crypto_handshake_init_with_password("crypto_client_local", &g_crypto_ctx, false,
323                                                  GET_OPTION(password)); // false = client
324     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
325       FATAL(result, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake with password");
326     }
327   } else {
328     // No password or SSH key - use standard initialization with random keys
329     log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Using standard initialization");
330     result = crypto_handshake_init("crypto_client_local", &g_crypto_ctx, false); // false = client
331     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
332       FATAL(result, "Failed to initialize crypto handshake");
333     }
334   }
335 
336   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: crypto_handshake_init succeeded");
337 
338   // Set up server connection info for known_hosts
339   SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.server_hostname, address, sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.server_hostname) - 1);
340   const char *server_ip = server_connection_get_ip();
341   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: server_connection_get_ip() returned: '%s'", server_ip ? server_ip : "NULL");
342   SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.server_ip, server_ip ? server_ip : "", sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.server_ip) - 1);
343 
344   // Use port from server_connection_set_port() if available (e.g., from WebSocket URL),
345   // otherwise use the port from options (TCP/default path)
346   int connection_port = server_connection_get_port();
347   g_crypto_ctx.server_port = (uint16_t)(connection_port > 0 ? connection_port : port);
348   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Set server_ip='%s', server_port=%u", g_crypto_ctx.server_ip, g_crypto_ctx.server_port);
349 
350   // Configure server key verification if specified
351   if (strlen(server_key) > 0) {
352     g_crypto_ctx.verify_server_key = true;
353     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key, server_key, sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key) - 1);
354     log_debug("Server key verification enabled: %s", server_key);
355   }
356 
357   // If --require-client-verify is set, perform ACDS session lookup for server identity
358   if (GET_OPTION(require_client_verify) && strlen(GET_OPTION(session_string)) > 0) {
359     log_debug("--require-client-verify enabled: performing ACDS session lookup for '%s'", GET_OPTION(session_string));
360 
361     // Connect to ACDS server (configurable via --acds-server and --acds-port options)
362     acds_client_config_t acds_config;
363     acds_client_config_init_defaults(&acds_config);
364     SAFE_STRNCPY(acds_config.server_address, GET_OPTION(discovery_server), sizeof(acds_config.server_address));
365     acds_config.server_port = GET_OPTION(discovery_port);
366     acds_config.timeout_ms = 5 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;
367 
368     // ACDS key verification (optional in debug builds, only if --discovery-service-key is provided)
369     if (strlen(GET_OPTION(discovery_service_key)) > 0) {
370       log_debug("Verifying ACDS server key for %s...", acds_config.server_address);
371       uint8_t acds_pubkey[32];
372       asciichat_error_t verify_result =
373           discovery_keys_verify(acds_config.server_address, GET_OPTION(discovery_service_key), acds_pubkey);
374       if (verify_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
375         log_error("ACDS key verification failed for %s", acds_config.server_address);
376         return -1;
377       }
378       log_debug("ACDS server key verified successfully");
379     }
380 #ifndef NDEBUG
381     else {
382       log_debug("Skipping ACDS key verification (debug build, no --discovery-service-key provided)");
383     }
384 #endif
385 
386     acds_client_t acds_client;
387     asciichat_error_t acds_result = acds_client_connect(&acds_client, &acds_config);
388     if (acds_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
389       log_error("Failed to connect to ACDS server at %s:%d", acds_config.server_address, acds_config.server_port);
390       return -1;
391     }
392 
393     // Perform SESSION_LOOKUP to get server's identity
394     acds_session_lookup_result_t lookup_result;
395     acds_result = acds_session_lookup(&acds_client, GET_OPTION(session_string), &lookup_result);
396     acds_client_disconnect(&acds_client);
397 
398     if (acds_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || !lookup_result.found) {
399       log_error("ACDS session lookup failed for '%s': %s", GET_OPTION(session_string),
400                 lookup_result.found ? "session not found" : "lookup error");
401       return -1;
402     }
403 
404     // Convert server's Ed25519 public key (32 bytes) to hex string
405     char server_key_hex[65]; // 32 bytes * 2 + null terminator
406     for (size_t i = 0; i < 32; i++) {
407       SAFE_SNPRINTF(&server_key_hex[i * 2], 3, "%02x", lookup_result.host_pubkey[i]);
408     }
409     server_key_hex[64] = '\0';
410 
411     // Set expected server key for verification during handshake
412     g_crypto_ctx.verify_server_key = true;
413     SAFE_STRNCPY(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key, server_key_hex, sizeof(g_crypto_ctx.expected_server_key) - 1);
414     log_debug("ACDS session lookup succeeded - server identity will be verified");
415     log_debug("Expected server key (from ACDS): %s", server_key_hex);
416   }
417 
418   // Mark initialization as complete
419   lifecycle_init(&g_crypto_lc, "client_crypto");
420 
421   log_debug("Client crypto handshake initialized");
422   log_debug("CLIENT_CRYPTO_INIT: Initialization complete");
423   return 0;
424 }






References acds_client_config_init_defaults(),
    acds_client_connect(), acds_client_disconnect(),
    acds_session_lookup(), options_state::address,
    ASCIICHAT_OK, crypto_derive_password_key(),
    crypto_handshake_destroy(), crypto_handshake_init(),
    crypto_handshake_init_with_password(), CRYPTO_OK,
    crypto_result_to_string(), discovery_keys_verify(),
    ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE, options_state::encrypt_key,
    FATAL, acds_session_lookup_result_t::found,
    g_crypto_ctx, GET_OPTION,
    acds_session_lookup_result_t::host_pubkey,
    private_key_t::key_comment, KEY_TYPE_ED25519,

    log_error, log_warn, MS_PER_SEC_INT,

    options_state::port, private_key_t::public_key,

876                                        {
877   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_crypto_lc) || !crypto_client_is_ready()) {
878     log_error("Cannot initiate rekey: crypto not initialized or not ready");
879     return -1;
880   }
881 
882   socket_t socket = server_connection_get_socket();
883   if (socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
884     log_error("Cannot initiate rekey: invalid socket");
885     return -1;
886   }
887 
888   asciichat_error_t result = crypto_handshake_rekey_request(&g_crypto_ctx, socket);
889   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {

891     return -1;
892   }
893 
894   return 0;
895 }

    INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE, lifecycle_is_initialized(),
    log_error, and server_connection_get_socket().






bool crypto_client_should_rekey (void )


Check if session rekeying should be triggered. Check if session
    rekeying should be triggered




Returns


true if rekey should be initiated, false otherwise


Definition at line 862 of file
  src/client/crypto.c.


862                                       {
863   if (!lifecycle_is_initialized(&g_crypto_lc) || !crypto_client_is_ready()) {
864     return false;
865   }
866   return crypto_handshake_should_rekey(&g_crypto_ctx);
867 }






References crypto_client_is_ready(),
    crypto_handshake_should_rekey(), g_crypto_ctx, and
    lifecycle_is_initialized().
